{{Infobox character
|nom=Dodo
|apparition=Appears in book 1
|status=Unknown
}}

== Biography ==

The Dodo is a creature of formal demeanor and practical initiative. Among the birds and animals that had fallen into the pool, the Dodo rose to take charge, speaking with solemnity. When the Eaglet challenged the Dodo's use of complex language, the Dodo responded with offended dignity. The Dodo proposed that a Caucus-race would be the best means by which the gathered creatures could dry themselves.

The Dodo marked out a race-course in a circular pattern and positioned all the party along its length. When questioned whether the precise shape mattered, the Dodo explained that it did not, suggesting a pragmatic approach to the undertaking.

== Relationships ==

'''[[Alice]]''' — acquaintance. The Dodo appears to interact with Alice as part of the gathering of creatures at the shore.

'''[[Eaglet]]''' — friendly_rivalry. The Eaglet's open criticism of the Dodo's formal manner of speech and the Dodo's defensive response suggest a competitive dynamic between them.

''Evolution:''
* [[Alice]] — Acquaintance (ch.3)
* [[Eaglet]] — Friendly rivalry (ch.3)
== References ==

- Alice's Adventures in Wonderland

[[Category:Characters]]
[[Category:Secondary Characters]]
[[Category:Personnages du Tome 1]]
